site.title: REZ Risk Radar
site.tagline: Emerging-risk signals across NSW's Renewable Energy Zones, read for the EnergyCo risk function.

digests.posture: Watch — a quieter weekend after last week's political surge, with no new acute triggers. The standout signal is AEMO's 2026 ISP capacity picture: Central-West Orana overtakes New England as the state's largest REZ at 14GW, while New England's project pipeline (11GW) now runs ahead of its planned 8GW network capacity.

Elevated — a political-plus-community pincer on the New England REZ still dominates: the Coalition's plan to cap the zone and scrap its 500kV Bayswater link, a 22,000-signature petition debated to a near-empty government bench, and the access-scheme consultation due to open into a hostile climate. A weekend counter-signal: a developer consortium is pushing AEMO to back a new far-west NSW renewables region.
Steady — community-benefit and logistics news, no action items.
Watch — landholder sentiment in New England warrants monitoring.
